name: Celsius to Fahrenheit Converter
description: Industrial-grade temperature conversion tool with precision validation, confidence adjustment, and actionable insights for process control and quality management.

inputs.label: Temperature in Celsius
inputs.businessContext: Process temperature reading from sensor or manual measurement. Must be above absolute zero.

inputs.label: Sensor Accuracy (±°C)
inputs.businessContext: Manufacturer-specified accuracy of the temperature sensor (ISO 17025 calibration standard).

inputs.label: Calibration Offset
inputs.businessContext: Field calibration adjustment to align sensor with reference standard (NIST traceable).

inputs.label: Target Unit System
inputs.businessContext: Select output temperature scale for downstream process or reporting (ISO 80000-5).

inputs.label: Enable Process Alerts
inputs.businessContext: Toggle threshold-based alerts for out-of-spec temperature conditions (Six Sigma control limits).

rules.message: Temperature cannot be below absolute zero (-273.15°C).
Sensor accuracy must be between 0.01 and 10 °C (ISO 17025).
Calibration offset exceeds ±5°C. Recalibrate sensor per ISO 9001.

thresholds.message: Temperature exceeds 100°C — risk of steam generation or material degradation.
thresholds.action: Inspect cooling system and verify process limits.

thresholds.message: Temperature below -40°C — potential for brittle fracture in materials (ASTM E23).
Sensor accuracy > 2°C — measurement uncertainty exceeds acceptable tolerance (Six Sigma ±3σ).

formulas.name: Adjusted Celsius
formulas.description: Apply calibration offset to raw Celsius input.

formulas.name: Fahrenheit Conversion
formulas.description: Convert adjusted Celsius to Fahrenheit using standard formula.

formulas.name: Rankine Conversion
formulas.description: Convert adjusted Celsius to Rankine (absolute scale based on Fahrenheit).

formulas.name: Kelvin Conversion
formulas.description: Convert adjusted Celsius to Kelvin (SI base unit).

formulas.name: Measurement Uncertainty
formulas.description: Calculate expanded uncertainty at 95% confidence (k=2) per ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 (GUM).

formulas.name: Data Confidence Score
formulas.description: Normalized confidence based on sensor accuracy (1 = perfect, 0 = unusable).

formulas.name: Primary Converted Temperature
formulas.description: Final output temperature in the selected unit system.
formulas.formula: CASE unit_system WHEN 'Fahrenheit' THEN fahrenheit_conversion WHEN 'Rankine' THEN rankine_conversion WHEN 'Kelvin' THEN kelvin_conversion END

primary.label: Converted Temperature
primary.description: Temperature in the target unit system with calibration offset applied.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Sensor Drift Risk
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Uncalibrated sensors may drift >1°C/year, causing hidden quality losses (ISO 10012).

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Rounding Error Accumulation
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Repeated conversions without significant figures can introduce up to 0.5°C error in batch processes.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Unit Mismatch in Reporting
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Mixing Fahrenheit and Celsius in reports leads to misinterpretation and rework (Lean waste).

suggestedActions.label: Recalibrate Sensor
suggestedActions.description: If sensor_accuracy > 1°C, schedule recalibration per ISO 9001 clause 7.1.5.

suggestedActions.label: Implement Statistical Quality Control
suggestedActions.description: Use control charts (X-bar and R) to monitor temperature variation and reduce hidden losses.

suggestedActions.label: Standardize Unit Usage
suggestedActions.description: Adopt a single temperature scale across all documentation to eliminate conversion errors (WERC best practice).

suggestedActions.label: Audit Calibration Log
suggestedActions.description: Review calibration records for all sensors in the process loop to ensure traceability (ISO 17025).
